full_text:
Severe heatwave alert today in Rajasthan:Temperature crosses 45°C in 9 cities; water crisis in 200 towns and villages
- Copy link
Temperatures in Rajasthan are now rising not only during the day but also at night. The Meteorological Department issued an orange alert for five districts and a yellow alert for another five districts on Tuesday.
The spell of extreme heat during both daytime and nighttime is expected to continue throughout the week. Over the past 24 hours, severe heatwave conditions prevailed across the state. Maximum temperatures in nine cities were recorded between 45°C and 47°C.
Chittorgarh and Pilani recorded the highest daytime temperature at 46.2°C. Meanwhile, Sikar and Udaipur witnessed the hottest day of the season so far.
As temperatures continue to rise, the water crisis has also deepened. Out of 315 cities and towns in the state, 205 are facing a drinking water shortage. Water is being supplied once every three days in 30 cities and towns, while 24 cities and towns are receiving water only once every four days.
Picture of heat in Rajasthan-
These cities were the hottest
Water crisis deepens even in cities like Jaipur
Water supply has been severely affected across the state. In 30 cities and towns, water is being supplied once every three days, while in 24 places it is reaching residents once every four days.
Even in major cities such as Jaipur and Ajmer, water is not reaching tail-end areas. The state government has sanctioned an emergency budget of ₹1 crore for each district collector. To tackle the drinking water crisis, the Water Supply Department has deployed 575 teams across Rajasthan.
Mercury crosses 40°C in all cities except Mount Abu
Maximum temperatures above 40°C were recorded in every city of Rajasthan except Mount Abu. In eastern Rajasthan districts including Karauli, Dausa and Alwar, temperatures ranged between 43°C and 45°C.
The worst impact of the heatwave was witnessed in western districts such as Ganganagar, Bikaner, Phalodi, Jodhpur, Jaisalmer and Barmer, along with Chittorgarh. The Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for heatwave conditions in the Kota division for the next three to four days.
Weather conditions in major Rajasthan cities
Jaipur
Severe heat and heatwave conditions persisted throughout Monday. The maximum temperature rose by 1 degree to 43.6°C. This was the fourth day this season when Jaipur recorded a temperature above 43°C. Nights also remained extremely hot, with the minimum temperature recorded at 30.4°C.
Udaipur
The hottest day of the season was recorded in Lake City. According to the Dabok Meteorological Centre, Udaipur’s temperature reached 43.3°C. Earlier, on 12 May, the maximum temperature had touched 43.1°C. On Monday, the city recorded a maximum temperature of 43.3°C and a minimum of 29°C.
Ajmer
Intense heat continued in the city. On Monday, the maximum temperature was recorded at 43°C, while the minimum stood at 30.4°C. This is the highest temperature recorded in the city this month. Water coolers have been installed at various public places to provide relief to residents.
Alwar
The temperature in Alwar has climbed to nearly 45°C. Scorching heat prevailed on Monday afternoon, leaving markets largely deserted. Temperatures have been rising continuously in the district for the past two days. According to the Meteorological Department, the heat is expected to intensify further in the coming days.
Sikar
Monday was the hottest day of the season in Sikar. Intense sunshine and heatwave winds troubled residents throughout the day. The daytime temperature crossed 45°C, while hot winds also continued during the night. People remained distressed even by evening after enduring strong hot winds all day.
Heatwave conditions are expected to continue for the next five days.
Kota
The impact of the intense heat continues to increase in Kota. The maximum temperature on Monday was recorded at 45.3°C. Strong sunshine and hot winds from the morning itself made conditions extremely uncomfortable, while roads and public places appeared deserted.
Jodhpur
On Monday, the maximum daytime temperature in Jodhpur was recorded at around 43°C.
According to the Meteorological Department, humidity levels are also expected to rise over the next seven days. Light cloud cover is likely during this period, although there is currently no forecast of rainfall.
